Qs.Headline & Outlet Metadata:
List only verifiable facts:
• Headline text
• Publication/outlet name
• Publication date (YYYY‑MM‑DD or "Unknown")
• Author(s)
• Section or series tag (e.g., Investigations, Projects)
• Outlet ownership/funding and known editorial stance

*Sample chain‑of‑thought:* “Outlet is ProPublica (non‑profit, donor‑funded). Article dated 2025‑03‑18 under the ‘Investigations’ banner …”
Qs.Author/Outlet Background:
Summarize context that could influence framing:
• Author expertise (beats, awards, prior investigative work)
• Past controversies, corrections, or retractions
• Outlet’s investigative track record and press‑freedom ranking
• Any major funding partners on this project (e.g., ICIJ, OCCRP)
If you cannot locate reliable info, write: "No additional background found."

*Sample chain‑of‑thought:* “Reporter won a Polk Award for 2019 tax‑evasion series; no significant retractions on record …”
Qs.Central Allegations:
State each allegation the investigation aims to prove. Use a numbered list; paraphrase concisely but preserve meaning. Add who is accused and the claimed wrongdoing.

*Sample chain‑of‑thought:* “1. Company X funneled $10 M through shell firms to bribe officials. 2. Bribery resulted in expedited mining permits …”
Qs.Evidence Matrix:
Create a markdown table:
| # | Evidence Item (doc title / quote) | Source Type (internal memo, public filing, interview…) | First‑hand? (Original/Secondary) | Corroborated by ≥1 independent source? (Y/N) |
Include every piece the article cites or links.

*Sample chain‑of‑thought:* “Leaked email from CFO → internal doc → Original → corroborated by whistleblower interview → Y …”
Qs.Anonymity & Corroboration:
Explain use of anonymous or confidential sources:
• Number of anon sources and their roles (e.g., ex‑employee, regulator)
• Reporter justification for granting anonymity (as stated or implied)
• Steps taken to corroborate their claims (matching documents, second witness, etc.)

*Sample chain‑of‑thought:* “Two former engineers spoke under condition of anonymity due to NDA. Journalists matched their timelines to SEC filings …”
Qs.Right‑of‑Reply:
Detail how subjects of the allegations were approached:
• Did journalists request comment? Y/N
• Response types (on‑the‑record, no comment, legal threat, denial)
• Placement of responses in the article (early, buried, absent)

*Sample chain‑of‑thought:* “Company issued a two‑sentence denial emailed to reporter; quoted in paragraph 5 …”
Qs.Legal / Ethical Review:
Note any indications of legal vetting or ethical safeguards:
• Mention of review by newsroom legal counsel
• FOIA or court‑record sourcing
• Redactions to protect identities or ongoing investigations
• Adherence to SPJ/IPC or other investigative guidelines
If not discussed, state: "No legal/ethical review mentioned."

*Sample chain‑of‑thought:* “Article cites FOIA request numbers and notes excerpts cleared by counsel before publication …”
